23.summary: The template has a solid security and ops foundation: APP_DEBUG defaults to false in config/app.php, HSTS is production-only, CSP is enforced in production, security headers are comprehensive, legal pages (Privacy + Terms) exist with routes, cookie consent is implemented, and the deploy script enforces APP_DEBUG=false and SESSION_ENCRYPT=true checks at deploy time. Critical gaps are: Sentry is not installed (only in 'suggest', not in composer.lock), so error tracking is opt-in post-purchase; the legal pages contain placeholder content that must be replaced before launch; LOG_LEVEL defaults to debug with no production guidance to change it; CORS is pre-configured to localhost:5173 (wrong for production); no database backup strategy exists; and no CD pipeline (all deploys are manual). The CSP defaults to report-only mode in non-production, correctly switching to enforced in production. The deploy script lacks a rollback mechanism.
p0_blockers: Sentry error tracking is not installed — it is only in composer.json 'suggest', not in composer.lock. Any production errors will be silent unless the operator manually installs it.

Legal pages (Privacy Policy and Terms of Service) contain placeholder template content with a visible destructive alert warning 'Do Not Use As-Is' — shipping this to production exposes legal liability and regulatory risk (GDPR, CCPA).
first_24h_watchlist: Verify SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN and VITE_SENTRY_DSN are set — Sentry is not installed by default and errors will be silent
Confirm mail is working: MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS defaults to support@example.com and MAIL_HOST is commented out — transactional emails (verification, billing) will silently fail
Verify queue workers are running: PersistAuditLog and all billing jobs require a running worker — deploy script sends restart signal but does not confirm workers are live
Replace placeholder legal content in Privacy Policy and Terms of Service before any real users access the app
Set LOG_LEVEL=error (or warning) in production — current default is debug, which writes verbose output including potential PII to disk
Update CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS from localhost:5173 to production domain — API is currently configured to reject cross-origin requests from the live domain
Confirm TRUSTED_PROXIES is set if behind nginx/Cloudflare — rate limiting and audit log IPs will be wrong otherwise
Database backups: no backup mechanism exists in the deploy or setup scripts — first 24h data loss risk is highest before backup schedule is established
Stripe webhook endpoint must be registered in Stripe dashboard and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET set before any subscription events will process
Set CSP_REPORT_ONLY=false explicitly in production .env — defaults correctly to false when APP_ENV=production, but confirm this is understood by operators

findings.title: Sentry error tracking not installed — production errors will be silent
findings.description: sentry/sentry-laravel is listed only in composer.json 'suggest' and is absent from composer.lock. config/sentry.php exists and .env.example documents SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN, but the package is not installed. Any production exception, including payment failures, auth errors, and unhandled 500s, will produce no alert. The VITE_SENTRY_DSN frontend DSN is also opt-in with no installed SDK.

evidence.note: sentry/sentry-laravel has 0 occurrences in composer.lock
'sentry/sentry-laravel' is in 'suggest', not 'require'
Config file exists but SDK is not present to consume it

implementation.approach: Run 'composer require sentry/sentry-laravel' and set SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN and VITE_SENTRY_DSN in production .env. The config/sentry.php file is already complete.
changes: composer require sentry/sentry-laravel
Set SENTRY_LARAVEL_DSN in .env.production
Set VITE_SENTRY_DSN in .env.production
verification: Confirm error appears in Sentry dashboard

findings.title: Privacy Policy and Terms of Service contain placeholder content — not legally valid
findings.description: Both /privacy and /terms pages render LegalContent.tsx which displays a destructive alert 'Template Content — Do Not Use As-Is' warning that the text is a placeholder and must be customized by a legal professional. Launching with this content creates GDPR/CCPA liability exposure, particularly as the app collects IPs, user agents, UTM parameters, GA4 data, and Sentry telemetry per the disclaimer's own text.

evidence.note: Visible red alert with 'Template Content — Do Not Use As-Is' rendered on both legal pages
evidence.proof: AlertTitle text: 'Template Content — Do Not Use As-Is'

evidence.note: Privacy page exists and is routed at /privacy but renders placeholder
Terms page exists and is routed at /terms but renders placeholder

implementation.approach: Replace placeholder content in LegalContent.tsx with company-specific, legally-reviewed Privacy Policy and Terms of Service. Remove the TemplateDisclaimer component from both pages before launch.
changes: Replace TermsContent component body with real terms
Replace PrivacyContent component body with real privacy policy
Remove <TemplateDisclaimer> from Privacy.tsx and Terms.tsx
verification: Load /privacy and /terms — confirm no red alert banner is visible

findings.title: MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS defaults to support@example.com with no production mail host configured
findings.description: .env.example sets MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS="support@example.com" and MAIL_MAILER=smtp but MAIL_HOST is commented out. Production deployments that copy .env.example without updating these values will have transactional email (email verification, billing receipts, dunning, onboarding sequences) silently fail or be rejected due to the invalid From address domain not matching the sending domain.

evidence.note: MAIL_MAILER=smtp but MAIL_HOST is commented out; MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS uses example.com domain

implementation.approach: The deploy.sh REQUIRED_VARS check only validates APP_KEY, APP_URL, DB_CONNECTION, DB_DATABASE. Add MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS and MAIL_HOST to the required vars list, or add a validation that MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS does not contain 'example.com'.
changes: Add MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS and MAIL_HOST to REQUIRED_VARS in deploy.sh
Add note in .env.example that MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS must be updated before launch
verification: Send a test email after deploy

findings.title: LOG_LEVEL defaults to debug with no production override guidance
findings.description: .env.example sets LOG_LEVEL=debug with no note to change it in production. Debug-level logging includes SQL query bindings (per config/sentry.php breadcrumbs.sql_bindings=true), request details, and stack traces. In production this causes verbose disk writes, faster log rotation, and may inadvertently capture sensitive request parameters in log files.

evidence.note: LOG_LEVEL=debug with no production comment alongside it
sql_bindings breadcrumbs enabled by default — debug log level amplifies exposure

implementation.approach: Add a production comment in .env.example recommending LOG_LEVEL=error. Add LOG_LEVEL to the deploy.sh production validation (warn if debug is set in production).
changes: Add # PRODUCTION: LOG_LEVEL=error comment in .env.example
Optionally add LOG_LEVEL!=debug warning in deploy.sh production checks
verification: grep LOG_LEVEL .env.production confirms error or warning level

findings.title: CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS defaults to localhost:5173 — API will reject requests from production domain
findings.description: .env.example sets CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:5173, which is the Vite dev server. config/cors.php uses this as the allowed origins list. In production, API requests from the real domain will be CORS-blocked. This would break Inertia partial reloads, Sanctum API calls, and any fetch-based calls from the frontend.

evidence.note: CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:5173 with no production override comment
Reads directly from CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var

implementation.approach: Add CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to the deploy.sh required-vars check for production, or add a comment in .env.example that this must be updated to the production domain. Also note that Inertia same-domain apps typically don't need CORS at all for web routes.
changes: Add # PRODUCTION: CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://yourdomain.com comment in .env.example
Add CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to deploy.sh REQUIRED_VARS for non-local envs
verification: curl -H 'Origin: https://yourdomain.com' https://yourdomain.com/api/user — should not return CORS error

findings.title: No database backup mechanism — no backup script, cron, or documented strategy
findings.description: scripts/ contains vps-setup.sh, vps-verify.sh, setup-horizon.sh but no backup script. vps-setup.sh has no backup cron setup. vps-verify.sh has no backup check. .env.example has no backup-related vars. For a SaaS app collecting user data and subscriptions, losing data in the first 24 hours due to a botched migration or disk failure would be unrecoverable.

evidence.note: No backup.sh, backup-db.sh, or any backup-related script exists
evidence.proof: ls scripts/ shows: _common.sh, check-bundle-size.sh, init.sh, setup-horizon.sh, test-horizon.sh, test-quality-check.sh, vps-setup.sh, vps-verify.sh

evidence.note: No backup cron entry added during VPS setup

implementation.approach: Add a documented backup strategy to vps-setup.sh: daily mysqldump cron to an offsite location (S3, Backblaze, or at minimum a separate disk). Add a backup check to vps-verify.sh. At minimum, document the backup requirement in the launch checklist.
changes: Add mysqldump backup cron to vps-setup.sh
Add last-backup-timestamp check to vps-verify.sh
Document in .env.example: BACKUP_S3_BUCKET or similar
verification: vps-verify.sh shows last backup was < 24h ago

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline — deploys are entirely manual with no automated deploy-on-push
findings.description: .github/workflows/ contains ci.yml (tests), dependabot-auto-merge.yml, and update-snapshots.yml, but no CD workflow. Deployments require manual SSH + deploy.sh execution. There is no deploy gate that blocks broken code from reaching production (CI passing does not trigger deploy). This is not a blocker for a solo developer but increases risk of deploy drift and human error.

evidence.note: No deploy.yml, cd.yml, or release.yml workflow present
evidence.proof: ls .github/workflows/ shows: ci.yml, dependabot-auto-merge.yml, update-snapshots.yml only

implementation.approach: Add a GitHub Actions deploy workflow that triggers on push to main after CI passes, SSHes to the VPS, and runs deploy.sh. Or document the manual process clearly in a DEPLOYING.md.
changes: Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml that depends on ci job success
Configure SSH deploy key as GitHub secret
verification: Push to main triggers deploy, vps-verify.sh passes post-deploy

findings.title: No deploy rollback mechanism — failed migration leaves app in maintenance mode
findings.description: deploy.sh runs migrations at step 8 with 'php artisan migrate --force'. If a migration fails after maintenance mode is entered (step 4), the script will exit (set -e) and leave the application in maintenance mode indefinitely. There is no trap/rollback handler to call 'php artisan up' on failure.

evidence.note: 'set -e' causes immediate exit on failure; 'php artisan down' at step 4 is not balanced by a trap
evidence.proof: Line 2: set -e; Line 227: $PHP_BIN artisan down; no 'trap' command found in script

implementation.approach: Add a bash trap at the top of deploy.sh: 'trap "$PHP_BIN artisan up; echo DEPLOY FAILED — app restored" ERR'. This ensures maintenance mode is exited even if a step fails.
changes: Add trap ERR handler in deploy.sh after PHP_BIN is set
verification: Intentionally break a migration and verify app comes back up from maintenance mode

findings.title: CSP defaults to report-only mode — no violation enforcement until explicitly changed
findings.description: config/security.php sets CSP report_only to true when APP_ENV is 'local' or 'testing', and false (enforced) in production. However, the .env.example comments say 'Set CSP_REPORT_ONLY=false in production to enforce CSP', implying operators may think they need to manually set this. If an operator sets APP_ENV=production but explicitly sets CSP_REPORT_ONLY=true, CSP is unenforced. Additionally, there is no CSP_REPORT_URI configured by default, so violations in report-only mode are silently discarded.

evidence.note: Logic is correct but relies on APP_ENV being exactly 'production'
CSP_REPORT_URI is commented out with no default — violations are silently discarded in report-only mode

implementation.approach: Update .env.example comment to clarify that CSP is automatically enforced when APP_ENV=production. Recommend a CSP report-uri service (report-uri.com or sentry CSP endpoint) so violations are captured before switching enforcement on.
changes: Clarify .env.example comment on CSP_REPORT_ONLY
Add CSP_REPORT_URI example pointing to a reporting service
verification: curl -I https://yourdomain.com — Content-Security-Policy header (not report-only) is present

findings.title: No email deliverability setup guidance — SPF, DKIM, DMARC not documented
findings.description: .env.example documents SMTP configuration but has no mention of SPF, DKIM, or DMARC DNS records. The app sends transactional email (verification, billing, dunning, win-back sequences) from a configurable From address. Without DNS email authentication records, email will be flagged as spam or rejected by Gmail/Outlook, particularly for billing-critical notifications like payment failure dunning emails.

evidence.note: SMTP section has no mention of DNS authentication records
No email deliverability documentation in docs/ directory

implementation.approach: Add a comment block in .env.example MAIL section pointing to the email provider's SPF/DKIM setup documentation. Recommend using Postmark, Resend, or SES which auto-handle signing when configured.
changes: Add SPF/DKIM/DMARC note in .env.example mail section
Note that Mailpit on port 1025 must not be used in production
verification: Send test email to mail-tester.com — score should be >= 8/10

findings.title: FEATURE_ONBOARDING config default (true) conflicts with .env.example default (false) — inconsistent documentation
findings.description: config/features.php sets 'onboarding.enabled' to env('FEATURE_ONBOARDING', true) — meaning if the env var is not set, onboarding is enabled. But .env.example explicitly sets FEATURE_ONBOARDING=false. This is not a runtime bug (the .env.example value wins when copied), but a documentation inconsistency that could confuse operators who read the config default and assume onboarding is off by default when it is actually on if the env var is not present.

evidence.note: Config default is true: env('FEATURE_ONBOARDING', true)
.env.example sets FEATURE_ONBOARDING=false

implementation.approach: Align config default with .env.example: change env('FEATURE_ONBOARDING', true) to env('FEATURE_ONBOARDING', false) in config/features.php, or update .env.example to FEATURE_ONBOARDING=true if the intention is that it should be on by default.
changes: Change config/features.php line 123 default to false to match .env.example
verification: Grep confirms both config default and .env.example agree on the same value

findings.title: Stripe Tax compliance flag is off by default with no pre-launch reminder — jurisdictional risk
findings.description: config/features.php documents 'PRODUCTION REQUIRED in many jurisdictions' for FEATURE_BILLING_TAX but defaults it to false. There is no pre-deploy validation that warns operators to check their Stripe Tax registration before enabling billing. An operator who enables billing (FEATURE_BILLING=true) without enabling tax may be non-compliant with EU VAT, US sales tax, or other jurisdictions.

evidence.note: Comment says 'PRODUCTION REQUIRED in many jurisdictions' but default is false with no deploy check
evidence.proof: // Tax compliance (PRODUCTION REQUIRED in many jurisdictions) ... 'tax_enabled' => env('FEATURE_BILLING_TAX', false)

implementation.approach: Add a warning in deploy.sh: if FEATURE_BILLING=true and FEATURE_BILLING_TAX=false, log a warning reminding the operator to verify tax compliance for their jurisdiction before launch.
changes: Add billing tax warning in deploy.sh when billing is enabled but tax is disabled
verification: Deploy with FEATURE_BILLING=true and FEATURE_BILLING_TAX=false — warning appears in deploy output
